signals.description: The modern wealthy family rarely lives, invests, and operates within a single country. A founder builds a business in Southeast Asia. Their children study abroad. Investment properties are acquired in different markets. A family office is established in Singapore. Before long, wealth exists across multiple jurisdictions, currencies, legal systems, and generations. This is creating a new challenge for affluent families. The issue is no longer simply wealth creation. It is wealth coordination. Many of the most significant risks facing global families today are not investment-related. They stem from fragmented planning, governance

source.value: The modern wealthy family rarely lives, invests, and operates within a single country. A founder builds a business in Southeast Asia. Their children study abroad. Investment properties are acquired in different markets. A family office is established in Singapore. Before long, wealth exists across multiple jurisdictions, currencies, legal systems, and generations. This is creating a new challenge for affluent families. The issue is no longer simply wealth creation. It is wealth coordination. Many of the most significant risks facing global families today are not investment-related. They stem from fragmented planning, governance
